top_signals.title: China Begins Tapping Oil Stockpiles as Middle East Crisis Drags On
top_signals.summary: China began tapping its huge oil reserves in May, three months after the Middle East conflict wiped out about a tenth of global supply, in a sign that Beijing is still refraining from paying top-dollar for prompt crude deliveries. Over the next few months, China is expected to draw an average of about 1 million barrels per day (bpd) from its massive oil stockpiles, according to estimates by energy consultants and energy flow-tracking firms Energy Aspects, Kpler, and Vortexa cited by Bloomberg. China is the world’s top crude importer, but…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Dubai’s DP World Meets Bond Investors to Allay Worries Over War
top_signals.summary: DP World Ltd., one of the world’s biggest container port operators, has been meeting with fixed-income investors to discuss its performance since the outbreak of the US-Iran war as the Dubai logistics giant mulls its options for repaying a bond due in a few months.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US, Iran Exchange Strikes, Putting Lasting Peace Deal at Risk
top_signals.summary: The US and Iran exchanged strikes overnight, jolting their two-month-old truce and endangering talks aimed at securing lasting peace in the Middle East.

top_signals.title: Posidonia China Night provides high-level platform for int’l maritime exchange, cooperation
top_signals.summary: Posidonia China Night, held in Athens during the Posidonia 2026, one of the world’s leading maritime exhibitions, brought together maritime industry stakeholders from China and Greece, creating a high-level platform for international exchanges and cooperation in maritime-related affairs. top_signals.title: CSSC begins construction of world’s largest LNG carrier for QatarEnergy
top_signals.summary: China State Shipbuilding Corp commenced construction on Tuesday on the world’s largest and most powerful liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier. The State-owned conglomerate said in a statement that the first in the QC-Max class, which has yet to be named, began construction at its Shanghai-based subsidiary Hudong-Zhonghua Shipbuilding, marking a milestone in a historic deal ...
